_comment: Constituency-level compass template. At constituency level, the 'parties' array represents INDIVIDUAL CANDIDATES (one entry per candidate). Use 'name' for the candidate's full name + party, 'short' for candidate surname + party abbreviation, and 'description' for their background. Theses should include both local constituency issues AND relevant state/national issues.

subtitle.en: Compare candidates standing for Constituency No. {N} – {Constituency Name} in the {Year} Tamil Nadu Assembly elections

text.en: Describe the constituency: location, demographics, key industries, voter profile. State that this compass compares local candidates (not parties) on local and regional issues. Note data sources.

parties._comment: One entry per CANDIDATE. The 'alias' field is used as the key in thesis positions. Use a readable slug like 'cand-dmk', 'cand-aiadmk', 'cand-ind'.

description.en: Background: profession, previous roles, community work. Core campaign focus areas. 2-3 sentences.

parties._comment: Add more candidates as needed. Include independents if they are significant.

theses._comment: Include 5-8 LOCAL issues specific to this constituency (roads, water, markets, slums, schools, hospitals etc.) and 3-5 STATE/NATIONAL issues representative of the broader election.

statement.en: Specific, actionable local policy statement. E.g. 'The {constituency} market should be relocated to decongest {street} and provide hygienic stalls for all 200 vendors displaced by the 2023 fire.'

explanation.en: What has this candidate specifically said or done about this? Cite a public statement, door-step commitment, petition filed, motion in council, etc.

theses._comment: State/national issue carried over from the state-level compass for context.

statement.en: Use the same statement wording as in the state-level config.json for consistency.

explanation.en: Party position. Individual candidate may amplify or moderate — note any deviation.
Party position with candidate-specific nuance if any.

_deployment_note: Place at: elections/{state}/constituencies/{constituency-slug}/config.json. URL: compass.html?config=elections/{state}/constituencies/{slug}/config.json. Add a card to elections/{state}/constituencies/index.html.
_data_quality_checklist: All candidate names are spelled correctly
All positions (approve/neutral/reject/skip) are backed by a cited source in the explanation
No explanation is blank — if unknown, use 'skip' and note why in a code comment
Both language keys are present for every multilingual field
Candidate descriptions are factual, not promotional
The introduction notes the data collection date and sources
